perceived_authenticity_bar.note: Students compare us to these — match the register.

generator_prompt_injection:
REAL TEST-TAKER INSIGHTS (sourced from r/ACT, updated 2026-06-12):
- Coverage: Real test-takers report this exam covers breadth over depth. Stay at college 101 level on each topic.
- High-emphasis topics (per real students): math, preparation.
- Students struggle most with: math, pressure from family, ela. Generate proportionally more on these.
- Wording style: ~6% of items should use realistic exam-style indirection (NOT unfair ambiguity).
- Difficulty target: 30% easy / 50% medium / 20% hard. avg pass score 32 suggests easier test.
- Authenticity bar: students compare us to Desmos, practice test, practice tests. Match that register.

popup_tips.text: On ACT MATH, real test-takers say breadth beats depth. Aim to recognize many topics at a 101 level rather than mastering a few in deep detail.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: Heaviest-weighted topics on the actual ACT MATH exam (per recent test-takers): math, preparation. Spend more time here.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: Where ACT MATH students reported struggling most: math, pressure from family, ela. Make sure you can handle these before exam day.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: Resources real ACT MATH test-takers credited: Desmos and practice test. Pair with practice questions.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT

popup_tips.text: A passing score on ACT MATH is reachable: recent successful test-takers reported scoring around 32 after focused study.
popup_tips.source_attribution: Compiled from real test-takers on r/ACT
